model.name: Creta
model.headerSeo: The 2025 Hyundai Creta facelift is definitely a top choice in India's mid-size SUV segment. It's got a bold exterior and a super modern, well-finished interior that's a treat to look at. Safety-wise, it comes with standard 6 airbags and a Level 2 ADAS suite with 19 features. You'll also get dual 10.25-inch screens, a voice-enabled sunroof, and a top-notch Bose sound system. The Creta's got three engine options: a refined 1.5L petrol, a reliable 1.5L diesel, and a punchy 1.5L turbo petrol. It's available with multiple transmissions, including DCT, IVT, and AT, making it a great balance of performance and efficiency.
model.pros:
The safety features on the Creta are pretty impressive. It comes standard with six airbags, electronic stability control, vehicle stability management, and a Level 2 advanced driver assistance system that covers 19 different safety features. 

In terms of comfort, I've got to say the Creta's got some nice touches. You'll find a panoramic sunroof that you can control with your voice, ventilated front seats to keep you cool on hot days, and an 8-way power driver seat for adjusting to your liking.

Now, let's talk about the tech. The Creta's got a pair of 10.25-inch screens - one for the infotainment system and another for the cluster display. And if you're an audiophile, you'll appreciate the Bose sound system with eight speakers. It's also got Bluelink connectivity, which allows you to connect your phone and access all sorts of features.

When it comes to the engine, you've got three options: petrol, diesel, and turbo petrol. And to go along with that, there are four transmission choices to suit your driving style.
model.cons:
Price Premium: The Creta's higher variants, which come equipped with ADAS and Turbo Multi-Clutch, do command a premium price compared to the base models. You'll need to budget a bit more if you're looking for these advanced features.

Rear Seat Width: The Creta's rear seat is spacious enough for two, but it can be a squeeze to fit three adults comfortably, especially when compared to some of its wider competitors. If you often find yourself carrying a full load of passengers, you might want to consider other options.

No Safety Rating (Facelift): As the latest facelift of the Creta has not yet undergone Global NCAP crash testing, we don't have an official safety rating for this new iteration. It's worth mentioning that the pre-facelift model did manage to secure a 3-star rating.
model.description:
The Hyundai Creta - this thing's got it all figured out in the mid-size SUV segment in India. Hyundai's given it a refresh, and it's looking sharper than ever with that 'Sensuous Sportiness' design language. Inside, you'll find a modern cabin, and the features list is seriously impressive - this is one of the best-equipped cars in its class. Safety's a top priority, too, with 6 airbags as standard and a full-featured ADAS suite (Level 2) that'll give you peace of mind on the road.

Under the hood, Hyundai's got a motor for every kind of driver. The 1.5L MPi petrol is smooth and refined, perfect for cruising around town. If you're looking to save some cash on fuel, the 1.5L U2 CRDi diesel is a great option - it's efficient, to say the least. And for those who want a bit more excitement, the 1.5L Turbo GDi is a real hoot. With three engine options to choose from, you'll be able to find a Creta that matches your driving style.

The Hyundai Creta's got a lot going for it, and it's clear why it's a best-seller in India. It's a well-rounded car that's got something for everyone - great looks, a comfortable ride, and plenty of features to keep you entertained on the go.
model.exteriorDesign:
The Creta's exterior is all about its 'Sensuous Sportiness' vibe. It's got a bold front end with a black chrome radiator grille and a sleek hood design that commands attention. The LED headlights are particularly eye-catching, especially the new LED lamps that are positioned in a way that creates a unique signature.

Moving around to the side, you'll notice the Creta's profile is defined by those 17-inch diamond-cut alloy wheels, which are a nice touch. Out back, the LED taillights are connected and give the car a sleek, aerodynamic look that's enhanced by the sporty spoiler. Overall, it's a pretty robust and muscular appearance that's sure to turn heads.

The dimensions are pretty impressive too - it's a compact SUV that's still got plenty of presence on the road. It's about 4,270 mm long, 1,790 mm wide, and 1,620 mm tall, making it a great size for navigating tight city streets or hitting the open road.
model.comfortConvenience: Step inside the Creta and you'll find a dual-tone grey and black interior that looks and feels solid. The driver's cockpit has been modernized with a sleek, curved display that's integrated seamlessly into the dashboard. It's all about comfort, and the Creta delivers – the driver's seat adjusts in eight different ways, there are ventilated front seats for hot climates, and a cooled glovebox to keep your drinks cold. The rear passengers aren't left out either; they get their own air vents, charging ports, and a center armrest with cupholders. The seats even recline in two steps, making long trips much more bearable. And with a large panoramic sunroof, the cabin feels airy and spacious.
model.summary:
So you're looking at the Hyundai Creta, right? It's a solid mid-size SUV with a pretty impressive feature list. I mean, the interior space is generous, and you'll find a ton of amenities, like blind-spot monitoring, 360-degree camera, and even a panoramic sunroof.

The driving experience is pretty good, to be honest. The 1.5-litre petrol and diesel engines are both respectable options, and the automatic and manual gearboxes are smooth. What's more, it's not like one engine is a clear winner over the other - both handle pretty well. It's a decent choice, and the quality feels consistent throughout.

engineSummaries.title: 1.5L MPi Petrol
engineSummaries.summary: Refined and linear naturally aspirated engine, perfect for city commutes and relaxed highway cruising.

engineSummaries.title: 1.5L U2 CRDi Diesel
engineSummaries.summary: A torquey and highly fuel-efficient diesel workhorse, ideal for high-mileage users and highway runs.

engineSummaries.title: 1.5L Turbo GDi Petrol
engineSummaries.summary: High-performance engine delivering punchy acceleration and sporty dynamics for the enthusiast.

faqs.question: What is the mileage of the Hyundai Creta?
faqs.answer: The Hyundai Creta offers excellent mileage ranging from 17.4 kmpl for the petrol manual to 21.8 kmpl for the diesel manual variant.

faqs.question: Does the Hyundai Creta come with a sunroof?
faqs.answer: Yes, the higher variants of the Hyundai Creta (SX and above) feature a smart voice-enabled panoramic sunroof.

faqs.question: Is the Hyundai Creta safe?
faqs.answer: The new Creta is equipped with over 70 advanced safety features, including 6 airbags as standard, Electronic Stability Control (ESC), and Level 2 ADAS with 19 features like Forward Collision Warning and Lane Keep Assist.

faqs.question: What are the engine options available in Creta?
faqs.answer: The Creta is available with three engine options: a 1.5L MPi Petrol, a 1.5L U2 CRDi Diesel, and a powerful 1.5L Turbo GDi Petrol.

variant.name: E Petrol MT
variant.description: The base E variant is ideal for practical buyers who want Creta's proven reliability and spacious interior at the most accessible price point. It includes essential safety features like ABS with EBD, dual airbags, and LED headlamps.
variant.keyFeatures: Dual Airbags, ABS with EBD, Rear Parking Sensors, LED Headlamps, Manual AC, Steel Wheels
variant.headerSummary: The Hyundai Creta E Petrol MT is the most affordable variant, offering essential features like dual airbags, ABS, and LED lighting. Perfect for buyers seeking Creta's spacious cabin and stylish design at an entry-level price.
variant.exteriorDesign: LED quad beam headlamps with LED DRLs, body-colored bumpers, halogen fog lamps, LED tail lamps, 16-inch steel wheels with wheel covers, shark fin antenna.
variant.comfortConvenience: Manual air conditioning, power steering with tilt adjustment, front power windows, manual day/night IRVM, central locking.
variant.engineSummary: The 1.5-litre MPi petrol engine delivers 113 Bhp of power and 143.8 Nm of torque. Paired with a 6-speed manual transmission, it offers a good balance of performance and fuel efficiency for daily commutes.
